Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson. We're welcoming you back to the 10X Secrets Training. Now today's session is one that I wanted to put together because I know that a lot of you guys who are either gone through this training or I got a lot of people read the expert secrets book and they're like, Russell, this webinar is really good. It works for people who are selling information products, but my business is different. I'm selling a physical product. I'm selling services or I'm selling, they have a million reasons why it won't work for them. I think human nature, the biggest thing we always try to do is we look at something new and write, oh, this won't work for me because of this. We have all these excuses that keep us from actually doing the work we need to do to figure out how this could work for me. I want to share this interview. This is with Jamie Cross and Jamie is someone who is now in my inner circle. She's someone who's gone through this training and she's someone who probably should have looked at this stuff I'm sharing with you guys right now and said, you know, this is not going to work for me because Russell, I sell soap. I'm selling soap at farmers markets. There's no way a webinar is going to work for me, but luckily for her and for me and for you, she didn't look at it that way. She said, how can this work for me? I'm going to tell you her story, but we're going to jump in the interview here in a minute, but she was literally at farmer markets every single week for months selling these soaps that she made, these custom organic soaps and things like that. She was doing that. She was learning what got people to buy, what kept them from buying and things like that. She started really understanding her customers, but she was struggling. She wasn't making enough money to really grow and have us as a real business for herself and for her family. She came to Funnel Hacking Live a couple of years ago and she came to the very first Funnel Hacking Live in the audience, completely broke, and I was talking about webinars. She could have heard that and be like, I'm selling soap, Russell, at farmer's markets. This isn't going to work for me. But she thought, I've got to figure out how this is going to work for me. She took the framework, the exact same framework that I've given you guys. I'll map out the framework. You guys have seen this a million times. This is the framework of the perfect webinar where you start with your intro, and then you've got your one thing and your three secrets. Then inside of there, then you go deep and you teach about it. You've got all your closes and you've got everything else. She took the same framework that you guys have been working off of for the perfect webinar. She said, okay, how can I do that? She figured out with my soap, what is the one thing about my soap that's unique, that's different than anything else? She realized that her big dominer, her one thing, her thing that's different from everything else, it happened to be the fact that if you put water in your soaps, you put all these other chemicals to stabilize the water. Her products are different because instead of putting in water, which means you have all these chemicals, she uses beeswax. That was her big thing, her one thing, her big secret that she wanted to share. Then basically she had three secrets. She took the same framework and she started plugging in her presentations. Then she went out and she tried it initially and she tried to do a Facebook Live webinar. She did a Facebook Live webinar. It was 30 minutes long and it bombed. It did not do very well. She said, oh, it didn't work. She tried it again. She tried it again. Maybe it's just, I'm selling a lower ticket product. Maybe I don't need the full 30 minutes to explain it or the full 90 minutes she's on a webinar. Maybe I can do it in a shorter compressed time. She's kept shortening and shortening and shortening it. Finally, she took the exact same framework and she did a webinar that's a little over five minutes long on Facebook Live and that was one that crushed it. I want you to understand it was the same framework. It was still, here's my one thing. Here's the big domino. I have to knock down. Here are the three secrets that helped me knock down that thing. Here's the offer I'm making, but she compressed it in time. What's interesting is that as you start learning this process, the first time I recommend everyone does it in a long form PowerPoint presentation. My new PowerPoint takes me 90 minutes. You saw it through the 10X Secrets. This is 90 minute presentation. If I go live on Facebook Live, I can take the exact same presentation. Just because of the format of how I'm presenting it doesn't really work. On Facebook Live, most people don't stay for 90 minutes. I take my same presentation and I can get the same presentation in 30 minutes by pulling out some of the stories and some of the examples, but the framework doesn't shift, doesn't change. If I wanted to do what Jamie's doing, I can take that same framework for 30 minutes, pull out some more things, shorten the stories and do it in five minutes. It's the same process, same script, same concepts. She's taking those things and compressing them for the format that you're in. For Jamie to sell her soaps, it was a five minute perfect webinar. That's the way you guys understand. It's really understanding the psychology behind this. She's using it for soaps. We have people now using it for every kind of product, physical products, e-commerce products, services, local chiropractors, dentists, things like that. People are still using it because the framework is the key. If you've gone through the 10X training, you've gone through the framework. If you've read the expert secrets book, you've read the framework. The framework is perfect. It's just figuring out how do you fit it in the time allotted for the product or service you're selling. I also want to share this one with you. If Jamie can use it to sell soap, and what's interesting is she went to Funnel Hockey Live and we talked with us in the interview. 12 months later, she was at Funnel Hockey Live again and her and her husband came on stage to get a two comma club award. They did that by taking this framework and it works. For any of you guys who are like, well, this product, this is not going to work for me or else because of blah and you have whatever the thing you're going to use to fill in the blank, I want you to stop. When you listen to Jamie Cross's interview, think about her and say, man, how would this work for my business? How can I take this framework to sell anything? I literally have people giving talks in churches and they're trying to convince at a congregation to repent or to do something. They use the same framework. I've got people who are using this in their emails. I've got people using some product launch secrets, people using it with their staff, with their team. The framework is the framework of how you sell to the masses. So don't discount the framework because you're selling something different. Instead, look at the framework that's perfect and plug your product, your service into it. And again, it might be a five minute thing. It might be a 30 minute thing. It might be a 90 minute thing. It might be a three day event. I do three or four day events. I use the same framework because the framework is the key. All right. So that's it. Let's jump into Jamie's interview. I hope you enjoy it and hopefully you'll see how you can take whatever you're selling and fit it into the framework you need to be successful with your product and your service online. Hey, everyone. This is Russell Brunson. I want to welcome you back to the 10X Secrets Training Course. And I'm excited to have somebody here today. Someone who I'm a huge fan of and a friend of. And her name is Jamie Cross. And I'm excited to have Jamie because everyone tells me when I first started talking about webinars that like, well, that's awesome, Russell, but like I am selling physical products. I'm selling things, you know, other things. And Jamie's super unique. She went and took this whole framework. We kind of started doing webinars and did it to launch her company selling soaps and oils and all sorts of different stuff. And so we're going to learn a little bit about behind the scenes, what she did, how she did. But thanks so much, Jamie, for hanging out with me today. Thanks, Russell. So excited to be here. So do you want to tell us first kind of the backstory about what your company is? And I, by the way, I'm, I use her product this morning in the shower. So I use it every single day. So I'm also a customer. But do I tell people what, what do you actually do? What do you actually do? Yes. So we do organic skincare. And we're kind of creating a whole new blue ocean with bio dynamic skincare now because we work with farmers and source everything from the soil. So from seed to skin where everything that you could need for your skin basically. He told people story about like, how did you, like, why did you start your business? Or how did that whole thing come about? Did you always want to be someone who made natural soaps and lotions and everything? Well, when I was a kid, we made everything from scratch. And I kind of lost all of those roots when I got into corporate. And when I had my first baby, I came home and like gave up a huge salary, gave a really great paycheck. And we were struggling financially and just struggling with like purpose. And I knew there was more. And as in that place of like just severe pain and okay, like motherhood is amazing, but I know there's more. So I started praying and I asked God for a billion dollar idea. It was like the cry of my heart. Like God, I want to do something that's going to change the world. I want to build something that I was born to build and I want to transform lives. And I prayed and I prayed and I remember we went to church like that Sunday and our pastor who is an entrepreneur stopped the sermon in the middle of his teaching and he's like, this has never happened before. But God just spoke to my heart and said that there's a stay at home mom here who he wants to give a billion dollar idea to so get ready for it. I remember still like I had my newborn, I had my cloth over my shoulder. I was nursing and I was like, God, that's me. Like, how are you getting all these people with it? Like just show me. And two weeks later I had a dream and it was like the whole business, the blueprint, the name of the company. I saw myself formulating and pouring these oils and I saw these amazing products and I just saw the whole thing. It's been eight years now and it still guides me. Like I still tap into that vision to build this thing and it's gotten so much bigger even since I started. So I love that. You felt that you prayed for it and then you heard it and I think a lot of people would hear that. I wonder who that's for. And you were like, this is for me. Like I'm ready. Like let me let me do this, which is so cool. Now after you know, you didn't, but you had to go and I'm, you had to go and like learn how to do all this stuff too. Like you didn't just, you had to go get certified. What was the process to be able to even be able to start doing it? Well, I woke up that morning and I was like, I think I'm going to start a skincare company and he's like, okay, go babe. And I was like, okay, what do I need? And here's what's interesting too. Like my dad was an entrepreneur and he would rarely give me answers when I ask questions. He was always like, go find the answer. Where can you find the answer Jamie? Like I was always finding and being resourceful and learning. So I had that drive to like, okay, what do I need to make something? I need to study chemistry. And I had failed chemistry in school. I was a really like poor student in high school, but I could, I could build a house. I could do all these amazing things. I started studying chemistry and herbal alchemy and essential oils and the skin and formulated our first product after about a year of like two way of nursing sessions with like my chemistry book and like my goggles eventually, you know, I was like in the lab breaking bad. I didn't even watch that show, but I hear. You get that, that's amazing. Yeah. So I studied for like a year and learned how to make my products. And then you guys started making your home, right? Initially? Yeah. So I was in the kitchen like making stuff and how to like, how to our first product ready to go after about a year and a couple months. And I just started hitting the streets. Like I walked up and down the streets of Denver, Colorado Springs. I was walking into stores asking for buyers, asking for owners with like my kit. And I was just doing on the spot presentations and closing like stores. And after about a year of doing that, really, I think there's something about a visionary like where you can see, you know, I was like looking ahead. Like I didn't like the fact that I didn't have control over the customer's experience. And I was losing profit by like selling my product wholesale to these stores and I couldn't like it wasn't my customer. And so we pretty much got out of that business model pretty early on, but I needed to do my research. I needed to know what the customers wanted. And so I got into farmers markets and spent four years talking to thousands of people and I formulated new products and failed in that area a couple of times. Like I made like 30 products that nobody wanted, nobody bought. But I kept formulating and then the stuff that was people were really gravitating to, I was really like formulating and reformulating. And so finally I had so many people coming back like this. I can't live without this product and this stuff healed my skin and I can sleep now and I can do all these amazing things. And so I knew that I was on the right track just by like getting in deep with people. The Farmarkets, did you have like your own booth at the Farmarkets or how did you guys do those? Yeah, so here we were like every Friday night. It was four days a week, the first year. And so we would load the car up. 4 a.m. I would drive to Denver and get it set up. So by 7 a.m. we had to be ready to go, ready to sound. It was rain, storm, shine, like it was raining out and I was under my tent like all my products. Whatever the case maybe it was just like grass, roots, table, products, like pitching out to people like hey do you want to try some body? Do you want a hand massage? Like I was doing all these pitches and like hooks and what do people want to hear? Like coming to my table because there were like four other skincare companies at the farmers market and I had to be the best. Like I had to create my blue ocean there at the market. And I did that first by raising my prices. So when people were like why is your soap $10 a bar? Everybody else is like $3 a bar so they'd be like what's so special about your stuff. But it was a whole journey of just figuring all of that stuff out like grass roots style. So cool because you have a chance to like test the product, find out the needs of the customer. All those things in such a cool way. I'm curious maybe it's not in my business to be like it's not in your business, Russell. Like when you guys were doing it that way, like how much revenue was coming into the business when you were doing the shows? Yeah, we were doing six figures every summer. So we had six months of farmers markets and we were doing about $120,000 every summer. But so much of that was like when I talk about not having money, it was because everything went back into the company. Like we were building and I was researching and creating and launching new things and it was expensive to be at these markets too. I was doing that, but I'm really doing it like more. That was what, that was two full life lives ago, right? Yeah. 2017 2017. And that was the one that we were launching expert secrets book right after we're so the whole event I was talking about webinars and all these things, right? It was like top of mind right then. Yes. I was starving for that book. Like I was like, when I think I bought on Amazon like months before it even came out, I was, yeah, I was like, take my money. It's crazy. And I was like, okay, I am going to go from being the formulator chemist salesperson to being a marketer. And when I made that identity shift, it was like, okay, I can do this. I can build my own funnel. I can find an ads person. I can do all the things that I thought, well, maybe I need to hire somebody because I had hired some people to do those things for me. And it didn't turn out while I was finding all these, you know, gurus that just were promising the world and not delivering. And so finally it was like, I can do this. I can build my own funnel. I love selling. I understand like how to create this flow. And I did. And my funnel is really ugly. Like you don't have to have this really good looking branded funnel. I think even that first inner circle meeting, you were like, oh, I need to help you. Can I please do something? Yeah, it's so bad. But we went from zero. And so here's an interesting part of my story too. We had moved. We had been in like this, my husband had been in this job position where we were building a ministry for like eight years and we got let go. And so we moved to Illinois for two years so he could train and like wrestle and coach and teach and do this stuff. So we were moving back to Colorado. I had been running our business from out of state. So this whole like click funnels going like this, like learning and our live transition of like coming back to Colorado. We were kind of like homeless for a month and like looking for a house and this massive transition like our infant had been hospitalized. But I was like every night I was like, I'm going to go build my funnel. And my mom's like, what is she doing? What is this funnel? Like I worked at the fair in high school and she's like, what funnel cakes? Like funnel cakes. And everybody's like, I don't know what she's doing, but she's like, I was in it. And like, I remember Garrett saying something about throwing your laptop out the window and I felt like that. It was severe, like just the pressure and the heat of like, okay, I'm going from here to here now, which is always a painful transition, but way better to go through that pain than to like go back to the mediocrity and to the, you know, what, where we were. And so here we were like figuring all this life stuff out and where are we going to live and where are we going to get a house and all this stuff. And we had no plan B. We had just enough money to last us like two more months. So it was like, this funnel better work or like I built the funnel. We made some tweaks. Like I made, so I was offering a bundle on the front end and did that for like a month and like nobody was buying myself. I was doing the perfect webinar over and over and over and over again. And it was like, nobody's buying my thing. I think we had one sale and it was like $200 for my skincare bundle, you know, the method. And finally I was like tracking things and looking and like we were getting emails and people like, I just want the lotion bar. And I was like, oh my gosh, duh. That's what everybody wanted at the farmers market. They want, if I could get them to buy the lotion bar, I could sell them everything else. And so I changed my offer and change it to the lotion bar. So we had one product on the front end and then we had, you know, our upsells into the bundle and within six weeks we went from like zero funnel, zero dollars to $130,000. And it was like, hair back, I joined in circles like, whoa. Russell said, I believe you. It didn't happen. It worked. It's so hard. Like that journey of figuring it all out. But it worked, you know, I still like, man, this thing works. That is so cool. Pinching myself every day, still like, man, this thing is amazing. That's so cool. I love your story. Now what I want, actually, I want to kind of step back and so what I'm most interested for the people who are buying this course now and going through this is they're learning about webinars and how to do webinars. And everyone that I talked to about webinars is like, oh, well, that makes sense. You're selling information product or coaching or like it makes sense in that realm because people have seen that so often. And first off, I'm curious why, because I think you were probably the first e-commerce person I met who was like, I'm going to do a webinar. And I'm curious, like, what was it in your head that you're like, that's what I want to do. And then I want to, I love the journey of, of like, what iterations you had to go on because you didn't get it. Yours isn't like a 90 minute webinar like mine is, right? It's completely different. I'm curious, like iterations you had to go to get to the, to the spot it is today. For sure. So I think anytime you start something like I followed your, I, I started out like it was all this, like I was like, oh, I didn't realize there was a segment between like digital and physical. I just assumed, okay, he's teaching the perfect webinar. I have to figure this out. So I was like highlighting everything that you taught in the expert secret book. I started to apply into my business. So the very first question was like, what's my big domino? Um, what is it that I'm doing? And one of our brand pillars is innovation and everything. And so I had already been thinking along, like, we had already built a foundation to be able to take the, um, the concept of the perfect webinar and turn it in, like apply it to our business. And so my first question is what was my big domino? And I think that's where I spent the most time because I know, and you write about this, once you have that, you know, your customers are like, they believe and then they'll throw their money at you. Basically, you know, they have no other option. And so, um, that was a real, like, I would say that took me a couple of months to figure out what exactly is my big domino and, um, what is that for you? He tells what, what the big domino is for you guys? Yeah. For what, for the first product for the lotion bar, it's our beeswax. It's the idea that if you have water in your lotion, which everybody does, it's dehydrating your skin. So you need a waterless lotion product that contains raw beeswax. And you might see beeswax in your skincare, but it's probably been imported and it's been bleached and all these things. And so I get ours raw, like fresh from the farm. So it does all these amazing things. I was able to build my three secrets around the benefits of raw beeswax and build down these, like the belief patterns of, well, you know, everything I've used doesn't work. So by the time they see our funnel or my, um, my webinar, it's like people have tried so many skincare products. They're really struggling. And so it's this idea of like, well, you've been using this water-based lotion. Now you need to step over here. Not only are ingredients innovative than big domino, but the way that you use our product is also innovative and big domino. So then once I had that, it was, it was really simple to like create the bold promise. And my opening line is, did you know that the most powerful ingredient for your skin doesn't come from plants? And so for my audience, it's like, well, what does work with my plants? Is everything essential oils, herbal oils, all these things that's, what are you talking about? Like, is it a toxin? Is it a chemical? So, um, so it was just a process of going from big domino to, okay, what are my three secrets? And, um, I did the perfect webinar, like for the 30 minute one. I hacked, um, Kalen's and I did exactly what she did. I was like, here's my things, like, you know, my paper and nobody was biting. Um, so I was like, okay, for a $30 product, I need to really condense this. And so I, um, I just shortened everything and I took out the stack and replaced it with urgency and scarcity, which we have legit urgency and scarcity. You know, like you can't just go and get a bunch of rubbish for every, you know, for eight pounds of honey that a bee will create. We get one pound of beeswax and like honey. So I used that and, um, and started testing that out. And then like, I think it was like the third video that I had done that was condensed, um, started converting, but I had already done like the 30 minute one quite a few times. Interesting. And then what, cause the price point you're selling on yours, how much is the lotion bar cost? The lotion is $30 was $30 offer. And how long does the, does the webinar take now? Will you watch it? Um, five minutes and I think 36 seconds. I have 36. That's awesome. And I watched it again this morning cause I was like, just kind of refreshing. And, um, I was like, this is not even that great. Like, if I did it now, I would, I would sound way more smooth, but I was still like, Oh, it was windy out. And I was like, wait, what am I saying next again? And so getting into a good rhythm from the information to from like, here's the information, here's the three secrets to now I'm going to make you an offer and like sell you something was a big thing to like, I practiced that part a lot. So it was an easy flow of, Oh, you know, okay, how do I get this product? Oh, no, here it is. That's cool. Trying to think of like my, which ones, which sequence, I guess if we started converting September 10th, that one would have been made in September because we like uploaded it and that one worked right away. That's amazing. Um, all right. So my question is for anyone who's listening to this and they again, they're, they're more like e-commerce side of business. They're not, they're not a coach, not a consultant or not whatever. Um, like if you were to walk them through the process, like what would be the, the process if you were coaching someone through how to do this for, for a physical product, how would that go? Yeah. I would tell them to first like take the expert secret book, um, and just map out, like figure out what your big domino is and write it out. Like I had 30 pages of white paper with like marker where I was like writing out my ideas and then practice it over and over and over again. Confidence will come with practice, but get your big domino. Once you have that, the three secrets will come naturally. And I would say do some hacking too. I mean, if you can look at a perfect webinar that's working, um, like mine, you can actually like replace your story, pop it in there. And, um, and then practice your transitions from like bold promise to introduction to three secrets to, okay, now I'm going to make the pitch. And if you can get that rhythm in there, um, that makes sense. Yeah. Super cool. Um, I love it. Did you know that the most powerful ingredient for your skin does not come from plants? I know it sounds crazy because we've been hearing about herbal oils, botanical extracts and essential oils for years. And while all of those are amazing and I use them for my skin every day, the truth is there's an even more powerful ingredient I'm going to share with you today and I've got three secrets. It's going to revolutionize the way you do skin and the condition of your skin. So I'm Jamie Cross. I'm a master herbalist and I've been helping men and women for over seven years now radically transform everything from eczema, psoriasis, dry, um, itchy, flaky, irritated skin to acne. So what is this mystery ingredient? It's very simple. Actually, it's raw beeswax. So why is that any different than what you're buying in the store when you see beeswax on the ingredients? The truth is, is that those ingredients that that beeswax has been imported. It's been bleached and denatured. It doesn't have any essence. It's completely white. So there's no vitamins left in it. If it has any value at all, it's very little. So raw beeswax, I know what you might be thinking. It's going to be sticky, messy. You know, how do you get access to it? I'm going to show you a way that you can help get all amazing benefits of raw beeswax without all the sticky mess. So the first amazing, powerful benefit to raw beeswax is that it's breathable and it's an antiseptic. So you can use it on your face and your body and it's not going to cause acne. It's not going to clog your pores. And it's, um, uh, especially because it is an antiseptic, it's going to help prevent future breakouts or if you have any infections on your skin, it's helping your body support its natural repair and healing process. The second thing is that it's humectant. So if you look at your lotion right now and you see water is the first or second ingredient, that lotion is dehydrating your skin. So water attracts water. And because of that, um, lotion products today are, are leaving skin lifeless, lackluster. It's the cause of many problems, you know, including acne or I'm sorry, including eczema and psoriasis. And I've seen this based on testimonial alone. So the humectant draws moisture from the air and it locks it into your skin. So when you're using lotion today, very little of that moisture actually gets into your skin. So the humectant, what it does is it draws it below the skin surface and it locks that moisture in. And of course, we know that's beneficial because soft supple skin means, um, radiance and vitality. And anytime there's moisture, which equals water in your skin, your skin is able to do what it naturally does. And the third thing is that it is regenerative. It's rich in vitamin A, so it's cushioning your cells and helping your cells to regenerate, just like they're supposed to do. So that means everything from, uh, if you have premature aging, it's going to help your skin get that youthful glow back. It's going to help protect and prevent future, um, you know, premature aging and then also revert any kind of, uh, you know, premature rink rinkles that you may have. So what's the best way to get access to Robby's wax? As an herbalist, um, I have access, I've been partnering with farms for years and I've created a lotion bar that you leave in your shower and you put it on right before your skin is still wet. So why is that powerful? I've combined everything from Robby's wax, which I get from Colorado farms here. I buy 40 pound block of bright orange and bright yellow beeswax that when we, um, when we make our products, our lab smelled like raw honey. It's just glorious and it's rich in vitamins, antioxidants, cause I combine it with herbal oils that are all organic, such as pomegranate and carrot seed and, um, blackberry leaf and I've infused nettles. And, um, you basically, what you do is you leave it in your shower and right before you get out while your skin is still wet, you just swipe, you just, um, roll it up, swipe some off the top and massage it in. Once you pat dry, you're literally done for the day. It's, um, it's infused and, um, absorbed into your skin by the time you get dressed. So there's no sticky mess. So I mean, and this is part of the whole like, like throwing rocks and polarity. The, the beauty industry is horrible. They use words like natural and safe.
